stories.headline: Banks push to slow stablecoin law as Agora races for federal charter
stories.tldr: US banks are lobbying to delay key stablecoin rules just as Agora pushes for a national bank charter under the GENIUS Act framework.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Bank pushback could carve out distribution lanes that lock crypto-native issuers out of T+0 settlement and Fed rails just as the rules are being written.
the_details: Bank groups want longer transition windows on reserve, capital, and disclosure rules tied to GENIUS Act stablecoin issuance.
Agora is racing for a national trust charter that would let it custody reserves and issue across the US under a single regime.
Treasury and OCC face pressure to finalize implementing rules before midterm political calendar tightens.

stories.headline: North Korea drove 76% of 2026 crypto theft, $6B stolen since 2017: TRM Labs
stories.tldr: TRM Labs says DPRK-linked actors account for 76% of stolen crypto so far in 2026, with cumulative theft since 2017 now over $6 billion.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: The concentration of risk in one state actor reframes hack defense from generic security hygiene to targeted nation-state threat modeling for every protocol team.
the_details: TRM Labs report attributes the bulk of 2026 losses to DPRK groups including Lazarus and TraderTraitor.
Bridges, custodial wallets, and admin-key compromises remain the dominant attack surface.
OFAC has expanded sanctions on mixers and OTC desks tied to DPRK laundering rails through April.

stories.headline: BTC, ETH, XRP slide as spot ETF outflows deepen heading into May
stories.tldr: Bitcoin, ether, and XRP fell Thursday morning as spot crypto ETF outflows extended a multi-day streak ahead of the Fed decision week.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Persistent ETF redemptions are draining the marginal bid that powered the 2025 leg up, leaving spot supply concentrated with weaker hands into a thin May tape.
the_details: BTC opened at $75,752 — its lowest open in over a week — before recovering to $76,130 by 7:18am ET.
ETH opened at $2,252, the lowest print since April 13, before edging to $2,268.
Crypto Times tracks consecutive net-outflow days across the major US spot BTC and ETH ETFs.

stories.headline: BTC closes worst April since 2022 but May seasonality flips bullish
stories.tldr: Bitcoin ended April defensive near $76K, but historical May returns and post-halving cycle data lean bullish according to CoinDesk traders.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A bullish seasonal setup colliding with weak ETF flows and shrinking volume sets up a high-variance May where positioning, not narrative, will dominate.
the_details: BTC has averaged positive May returns in post-halving years across the past three cycles.
Analysts cite 30-year yields near 5% and lingering Iran risk as macro overhangs to the seasonal tailwind.
BTC spot volume has been falling fast — a setup that historically resolves in sharp moves either direction.

stories.headline: Aptos leads CoinDesk 20 with 4.4% pop as nearly all index assets rise
stories.tldr: Aptos jumped 4.4% to lead the CoinDesk 20 higher Thursday, with nearly every index constituent posting gains despite weak BTC tape.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Alt strength while BTC drifts lower hints at rotation back into higher-beta names — the first such tape behavior since the April risk-off began.
the_details: APT led the index, with broad participation across L1s and infra tokens.
CoinDesk 20 covers the largest, most liquid digital assets ex-stablecoins.
Rotation tape contrasts with this week's $-DXY correlation that has dragged BTC.
